perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall mean the following: a) all such information, of any kind whatsoever (whether in oral, written or electronic form, and including, but not limited to, technical, commercial, financial, accounting, legal and administrative information) pertaining to the Sale of the Munt and the Sell ers as may be provided to the Disclosee and their responsible managers, officers, employees , shareholders, members of the Board of Directors and advisors (including financial, legal and tax advisors and auditors) (“ Representatives ”), by the Seller s, their advisors or their representatives; b) all such analyses, compilations, forecasts, notes, memoranda and other documents as the Seller s or Deloitte Real Estate * and Laga ** (the “ Advisors ”) may have prepared or caused to be prepared and which may contain, refer to or simply result from, the information mentioned in point (a) above; c) the fact that the Disclosee (or any of their Representatives) are or have been involved in the analysis of, in meetings or negotiations related to the Sale , the contents, time and sta tus of such negotiations, and generally any fact concerning the Sale .
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall mean the following: a) all such information, of any kind whatsoever (whether in oral, written or electronic form, and including, but not limited to, technical, commercial, financial, accounting, legal and administrative information) pertaining to the Sale of the Munt and the Sell ers as may be provided to the Disclosee and their responsible managers, officers, employees , shareholders, members of the Board of Directors and advisors (including financial, legal and tax advisors and auditors) (“ Representatives ”), by the Seller s, their advisors or their representatives; b) all such analyses, compilations, forecasts, notes, memoranda and other documents as the Seller s or Deloitte Real Estate * and Laga ** (the “ Advisors ”) may have prepared or caused to be prepared and which may contain, refer to or simply result from, the information mentioned in point (a) above; c) the fact that the Disclosee (or any of their Representatives) are or have been involved in the analysis of, in meetings or negotiations related to the Sale , the contents, time and sta tus of such negotiations, and generally any fact concerning the Sale. Confidential Information shall, under no circumstances, include information readily available on public domain or on the internet without specific confidentiality restrictions.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a clause stating that information readily available in the public domain is not considered Confidential Information, contradicting the initial broad definition which includes 'all such information, of any kind whatsoever.' This creates an ambiguity about whether publicly available information related to the Sale still falls under the agreement's confidentiality obligations as the initial definition is quite broad. In particular this would contradict 'Confidential Information shall mean the following: a) all such information, of any kind whatsoever'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall mean the following: a) all such information, of any kind whatsoever (whether in oral, written or electronic form, and including, but not limited to, technical, commercial, financial, accounting, legal and administrative information) pertaining to the Sale of the Munt and the Sell ers as may be provided to the Disclosee and their responsible managers, officers, employees , shareholders, members of the Board of Directors and advisors (including financial, legal and tax advisors and auditors) (“ Representatives ”), by the Seller s, their advisors or their representatives; b) all such analyses, compilations, forecasts, notes, memoranda and other documents as the Seller s or Deloitte Real Estate * and Laga ** (the “ Advisors ”) may have prepared or caused to be prepared and which may contain, refer to or simply result from, the information mentioned in point (a) above; c) the fact that the Disclosee (or any of their Representatives) are or have been involved in the analysis of, in meetings or negotiations related to the Sale , the contents, time and sta tus of such negotiations, and generally any fact concerning the Sale. Confidential Information shall, under no circumstances, include information readily available on public domain or on the internet without specific confidentiality restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause excluding publicly available information from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts the original and contradicted text's broad definition in section a) which states 'all such information, of any kind whatsoever'. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction, creating ambiguity and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Belgian law and the Parties irrevocably agree to submit themselves to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of Brussels.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Belgian law, except for matters concerning intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of the jurisdiction where the intellectual property rights are registered. The Parties irrevocably agree to submit themselves to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of Brussels.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding governing law. While the primary law is Belgian law, intellectual property matters are subject to the laws of the jurisdiction where the IP rights are registered. This contradicts the original statement that the entire agreement is governed exclusively by Belgian law and could cause disputes as to which law applies and also creates an in-text contradiciton with 'This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Belgian law'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Belgian law, except for matters concerning intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of the jurisdiction where the intellectual property rights are registered. The Parties irrevocably agree to submit themselves to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of Brussels.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law clause, introducing an exception for intellectual property matters. This directly contradicts the initial statement that the entire agreement is governed exclusively by Belgian law. The contradiction arises because the original agreement stipulates a single governing law for all aspects, while the modified version introduces another jurisdiction's law for IP, creating ambiguity and potential conflict. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, because it creates an in-text contradiction as per the explanation given.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Disclosee or any of its Representatives becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information to a regulatory authority or to any other entity or third party, the Disclosee shall immediately notify the Sellers before disclosing such Confidential In formation, so that the Sellers may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, without prejudice to t he Disclosee’s remedies under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Disclosee or any of its Representatives becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information to a regulatory authority or to any other entity or third party, the Disclosee shall use reasonable efforts to notify the Sellers before disclosing such Confidential In formation, so that the Sellers may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, without prejudice to t he Disclosee’s remedies under this Agreement. However, in cases where immediate disclosure is required by law to avoid penalties, immediate notification is not required.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires 'immediate notification' to the Sellers before disclosure. The modified clause changes this to 'reasonable efforts to notify' and adds an exception where immediate notification isn't required if it avoids penalties. This creates an in-text contradiction about the obligation to notify immediately and it goes against 'the Disclosee shall immediately notify the Sellers'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Disclosee or any of its Representatives becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information to a regulatory authority or to any other entity or third party, the Disclosee shall use reasonable efforts to notify the Sellers before disclosing such Confidential In formation, so that the Sellers may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, without prejudice to t he Disclosee’s remedies under this Agreement. However, in cases where immediate disclosure is required by law to avoid penalties, immediate notification is not required.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'immediate notification', but the changed text modifies this to 'reasonable efforts to notify' and introduces an exception for cases requiring immediate disclosure to avoid penalties. This directly contradicts the original requirement of immediate notification, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text which is the same as changed text now has an internal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: It is further understood and agreed that, without prejudice to any other rights or remedies which either party may have, any brea ch of these undertakings could cause the Sellers and/or its shareholders irreparable injury. In the event of an actual or threatened breach by the Disclosee and/or its Representatives of any provisions of this Agreement , the Sellers shall be entitled (i) t o seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction restraining the Disclosee and/or its Representatives from breaching the terms hereof or from disclosing any Confidential Information to any person and (ii) specific performance and other equit able relief.
perturbation.changed_text: It is further understood and agreed that, without prejudice to any other rights or remedies which either party may have, any breach of these undertakings could cause the Sellers and/or its shareholders irreparable injury. In the event of a material breach by the Disclosee and/or its Representatives of any provisions of this Agreement, after providing a 30-day written notice and opportunity to cure, the Sellers shall be entitled (i) to seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction restraining the Disclosee and/or its Representatives from breaching the terms hereof or from disclosing any Confidential Information to any person and (ii) specific performance and other equitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that any breach could cause irreparable injury and allows the Sellers to seek injunctive relief immediately. The modified text limits this to 'material breach', requires a 30-day notice and cure period. This creates a contradiction about the threshold for seeking injunctive relief, going against 'any breach of these undertakings could cause the Sellers and/or its shareholders irreparable injury' and the immediate right to remedies. It now needs to be a material breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is further understood and agreed that, without prejudice to any other rights or remedies which either party may have, any breach of these undertakings could cause the Sellers and/or its shareholders irreparable injury. In the event of a material breach by the Disclosee and/or its Representatives of any provisions of this Agreement, after providing a 30-day written notice and opportunity to cure, the Sellers shall be entitled (i) to seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction restraining the Disclosee and/or its Representatives from breaching the terms hereof or from disclosing any Confidential Information to any person and (ii) specific performance and other equitable relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the Sellers to seek injunctive relief for 'any breach,' implying immediate action. The changed text modifies this by requiring a 'material breach,' a 30-day notice, and an opportunity to cure before seeking relief. This directly contradicts the original immediate entitlement to injunctive relief, creating a conflict about when such relief can be sought, thus, a strong in-text contradiction.
